chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P No.D-301 of 2019 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on M.A 1708/19 2. For orders on office objection 3. For hearing of MA 1709/19 4. For orders on M.A 1710/19 5. For hearing of main case. 12.03.2019 Ms. Rehana Siddiqui, Advocate, holds brief on behalf of Mr. Bhagwan Das Bheel, Advocate for petitioner...... The petitioner is a civil servant working as Junior Clerk in Education Department and by means of this petition has challenged the order dated 14.11.2018 whereby he has been directed to report to office of Secretary, School Education and Literacy Department, Karachi. Learned counsel for the petitioner is called absent and on his behalf Ms. Rehana Siddiqui, Advocate, is holding brief. We have asked her to satisfy the court as to how a civil servant can file Constitutional Petition against his transfer order; she has no reply. In our view this petition is not maintainable on account of bar under Article 212 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973. If the petitioner is aggrieved by the impugned order, he can avail a remedy before the Service Tribunal. Accordingly, this petition is dismissed in limine being non-maintainable alongwith listed applications. JUDGE JUDGE Ali Haider
